english: The Goddess says she has heard the teachings arising from the Rudrayāmala, including the divisions of Trika and their essential gradations.

padas: अद्यापि न निवृत्तो मे संशयः परमेश्वर
iast: adyāpi na nivṛtto me saṃśayaḥ parameśvara
english: Her doubt remains: what is Bhairava in truth? Is the divine reality composed of the totality of sound and its kalās?
She continues testing possible definitions: is Bhairava the ninefold form, the three-headed division, or the triad of śaktis?

iast: cakrārūḍham anackaṃ vā kiṃ vā śakti-svarūpakam
english: The inquiry continues through subtle tantric categories—nāda, bindu, the half-moon, nirodhikā, cakras, the unvocalized, and Śakti herself.

iast: parāparāyāḥ sakalam aparāyāś ca vā punaḥ
parāyā yadi tadvat syāt paratvaṃ tad virudhyate
english: If the supreme is identified with the differentiated forms of Parāparā or Aparā, the Goddess asks, how can its unsurpassed transcendence remain coherent?

padas: न हि वर्णविभेदेन देहभेदेन वा भवेत्
iast: na hi varṇa-vibhedena deha-bhedena vā bhavet
paratvaṃ niṣkalatvena sakala-tve na tad bhavet
english: Supremacy cannot depend on distinctions of phoneme or body. The verse contrasts the partless, niṣkala reality with manifested, divisible form.

padas: प्रसादं कुरु मे नाथ निःशेषं छिन्धि संशयम्
साधु साधु त्वया पृष्टं तन्त्रसारमिदं प्रिये
iast: prasādaṃ kuru me nātha niḥśeṣaṃ chindhi saṃśayam
sādhu sādhu tvayā pṛṣṭaṃ tantra-sāram idaṃ priye
english: The Goddess asks for complete clarity. Bhairava praises the question as an inquiry into the very essence of Tantra.

iast: yat kiṃcit sakalaṃ rūpaṃ bhairavasya prakīrtitam
english: Though the teaching is deeply secret, Bhairava agrees to explain. He begins by referring back to every determinate form previously attributed to Bhairava.
Those determinate forms are not ultimate: they resemble magical display, dream, māyā, or an illusory city seen in the sky.
Such forms serve people absorbed in elaborate rites or conceptual constructions; they are supports for meditation, not the final reality.

padas: न चासौ त्रिशिरा देवो न च शक्तित्रयात्मकः
iast: tattvato na navātmāsau śabda-rāśir na bhairavaḥ
na cāsau triśirā devo na ca śakti-trayātmakaḥ
english: In truth Bhairava is neither the ninefold deity nor the aggregate of sounds, neither three-headed nor constituted by three śaktis.
The negation continues: Bhairava is not reducible to nāda and bindu, lunar stages, a sequence of cakras, or even a separately conceived Śakti-form.
These symbolic forms are provisional aids for unawakened minds, like a warning used for a child or a sweet offered to encourage action.
Ultimate reality is free from measurements of direction and time, not confined to any designated place, and finally beyond description.
Bhairavī is the inwardly known bliss of a state free from conceptual alternatives—a fullness belonging to Bhairava's own nature.

padas: एवंविधे परे तत्त्वे कः पूज्यः कश्च तृप्यति
iast: tad vapus tattvato jñeyaṃ vimalaṃ viśva-pūraṇam
evaṃvidhe pare tattve kaḥ pūjyaḥ kaś ca tṛpyati
english: Know Bhairava's true body as stainless awareness filling the universe. At that level, the divisions among worshipper, worshipped, and satisfaction lose their footing.
The supreme state of Bhairava is called Parā Devī because she is the highest power through which that transcendent reality is expressed.
Śakti and the possessor of Śakti are never separate. Power is the intrinsic nature of the supreme Self, not a second entity beside it.

iast: na vahner dāhikā śaktir vyatiriktā vibhāvyate
english: Fire's capacity to burn cannot be separated from fire. Likewise, speaking of Śakti is an initial doorway by which awareness approaches recognition.

iast: tadāsau śiva-rūpī syāc chaivī mukham ihocyate
english: One who enters the state of Śakti and contemplates without division becomes Śiva-like; therefore Śakti is called the face or entrance of Śiva.

iast: jñāyate digvibhāgādi tadvac chaktyā śivaḥ priye
english: A lamp is known by its light and the sun by its rays; similarly, Śiva is disclosed through Śakti and her differentiating radiance.
The Goddess addresses the trident-marked Lord and resumes her request concerning the full state beyond direction, place, time, and verbal designation.

padas: यथा सम्यगहं वेद्मि तथा मे ब्रूहि भैरव
iast: kair upāyair mukhaṃ tasya parādevī kathaṃ bhavet
yathā samyag ahaṃ vedmi tathā me brūhi bhairava
english: She asks which methods reveal Bhairava's all-filled state and how Parā Devī becomes its doorway, requesting instruction grounded in direct understanding.

padas: ऊर्ध्वे प्राणो ह्यधो जीवो विसर्गात्मा परोच्चरेत्
iast: ūrdhve prāṇo hy adho jīvo visargātmā paroccaret
english: Attend to the upward prāṇa and downward-moving jīva at the two points where they arise; their balanced interval discloses a state of fullness.
When the inner or outer breath pauses and the paired spaces are no longer traversed, Bhairava's form is revealed through Bhairavī.

iast: na vrajen na viśec chaktir marud-rūpā vikāsite
english: When breath-energy neither moves outward nor enters inward but rests expanded in the middle, nonconceptual awareness takes Bhairava's form.

padas: कुम्भिता रेचिता वापि पूरिता वा यदा भवेत्
iast: kumbhitā recitā vāpi pūritā vā yadā bhavet
english: At the end of retention, exhalation, or inhalation, contemplate the power called Śāntā; through her, the peaceful reality shines forth.
Visualize an ever-subtler ray rising from the root and subsiding at the dvādaśānta; its quieting becomes the emergence of Bhairava.

iast: ūrdhvaṃ muṣṭi-trayaṃ yāvat tāvad ante mahodayaḥ
english: Contemplate lightning-like energy rising successively through each cakra to the space above the head; at the culmination, a great awakening occurs.
Pass successively beyond the twelve stages and their corresponding phonemic distinctions, relinquishing gross, subtle, and supreme supports until only Śiva remains.
Fill the ascent swiftly to the crown, cross the bridge at the brows, and make the mind free of alternatives; the all-pervading source is then entered above.
Meditate on five voids as variegated circles like peacock feathers; entry into the unsurpassed void occurs in the heart.

padas: शून्ये कुड्ये परे पात्रे स्वयं लीना वरप्रदा
iast: śūnye kuḍye pare pātre svayaṃ līnā vara-pradā
english: Whatever the chosen support—a void, wall, elevated space, or vessel—let contemplation dissolve into it by itself; that absorption is fruitful.

iast: krameṇa manaso dārḍhyāl lakṣayel lakṣyam uttamam
english: Place attention inside the skull with eyes closed. As the mind becomes steady, the highest object of awareness can be discerned.
Meditate on the central nāḍī as a fine lotus-fibre thread filled with inner space; through this Goddess, the divine reality becomes manifest.

padas: दृष्टे बिन्दौ क्रमाल्लीने तन्मध्ये परमा स्थितिः
iast: dṛṣṭe bindau kramāl līne tan-madhye paramā sthitiḥ
english: Close the sensory gateways with the hands and focus on the point perceived near the brow; as that bindu dissolves, the supreme state appears within it.

padas: बिन्दुं शिखान्ते हृदये लयान्ते ध्यायतो लयः
iast: binduṃ śikhānte hṛdaye layānte dhyāyato layaḥ
english: Meditate on a subtle fire-mark like a sesame seed, arising from inner agitation of the luminous centre, at the crown, heart, and point of dissolution.
Absorb attention in an unstruck, continuous sound—like a river's flow heard at the inner ear. Mastery of śabda-brahman opens toward supreme Brahman.

iast: śūnyayā parayā śaktyā śūnyatām eti bhairavi
english: Prolong Oṃ or another sacred utterance, then contemplate the void at its ending; through supreme void-Śakti, awareness enters emptiness.
With any phoneme, experience both its beginning and its end. Attending through the intervening void makes the practitioner void-like.
Listen one-pointedly to the sustained tones of stringed instruments or similar sounds; at each sound's ending, awareness assumes the form of the supreme space.
In a mantra's full phonemic unfolding, follow sound through half-moon, bindu, and nāda into the final void; the voided utterance culminates in Śiva.
Contemplate space simultaneously extending in every direction within your own body. For a mind free of alternatives, all appears as space.

iast: pṛṣṭa-śūnyaṃ mūla-śūnyaṃ yugapad bhāvayec ca yaḥ
english: Contemplate at once the void above and the void at the root; body-independent Śakti then leaves the mind empty of constructions.
Hold steadily the upper, root, and heart voids together. Their simultaneous, nonconceptual apprehension gives rise to the nonconceptual state.
For even a moment, contemplate a chosen region of the body as sheer emptiness. A nonconceptual practitioner thereby shares the nature of the nonconceptual.

iast: vibhāvayet tatas tasya bhāvanā sā sthirā bhavet
english: Contemplate every substance composing the body as pervaded by space; sustained practice stabilizes this recognition.

iast: na kiṃcid antare tasya dhyāyann adhyeya-bhāg bhavet
english: Regard the skin as only a boundary around the body and contemplate nothing within it; the meditator becomes free of a separate object of meditation.
With senses withdrawn into the heart's inner space, abide one-pointedly within the closed lotus-bud; the verse points toward supreme spiritual felicity.
Dissolve mind everywhere around the body's dvādaśānta. With firm understanding, the intended reality becomes firmly established.

padas: यथा तथा यत्र तत्र द्वादशान्ते मनः क्षिपेत्
iast: yathā tathā yatra tatra dvādaśānte manaḥ kṣipet
english: Cast the mind repeatedly into the dvādaśānta, wherever and however possible; as mental activity diminishes moment by moment, a distinct state develops.

iast: pluṣṭaṃ vicintayed ante śāntābhāsas tadā bhavet
english: Imagine the city of your embodied identity consumed by the fire of time rising from time's own ground; when the burning ends, peaceful luminosity remains.

iast: evam eva jagat-sarvaṃ dagdhaṃ dhyātvā vikalpataḥ
english: Likewise contemplate the whole universe as burned away. With undivided attention, limited personal identity gives way to the supreme condition.

padas: तत्त्वानि यानि निलयं ध्यात्वान्ते व्यज्यते परा
iast: tattvāni yāni nilayaṃ dhyātvānte vyajyate parā
english: Contemplate the increasingly subtle tattvas of your body or the universe dissolving into their source; at the end, Parā becomes manifest.

padas: पीनां च दुर्बलां शक्तिं ध्यात्वा द्वादशगोचरे
iast: pīnāṃ ca durbalāṃ śaktiṃ dhyātvā dvādaśa-gocare
english: This verse describes a subtle Śakti practice involving contrasting fullness and weakness at the twelvefold field, then entry into the heart; its precise technique is textually compressed.
Contemplate the entire sequence of worlds and paths, moving through gross, subtle, and supreme levels until the mind dissolves at the end.
Meditate around every boundary of the universe through the ordered adhvan scheme; recognition of the Śaiva reality brings a great arising.

iast: tatraiva ca mano līnaṃ tatas tal-laya-bhājanam
english: Contemplate the whole universe as empty and let the mind dissolve in that very emptiness; awareness becomes a vessel for dissolution.

iast: tal-layaṃ tat-kṣaṇād gatvā tal-layāt tan-mayo bhavet
english: Look into a pot or similar container while disregarding its enclosing walls. When attention merges with the interior space, it becomes of that nature.
Direct the gaze toward a treeless, wall-less expanse such as an open mountainside. As the mental object dissolves, thought-waves diminish.

padas: युगपच्च द्वयं त्यक्त्वा मध्ये तत्त्वं प्रकाशते
iast: ubhayor bhāvayor jñāne dhyātvā madhyaṃ samāśrayet
yugapac ca dvayaṃ tyaktvā madhye tattvaṃ prakāśate
english: When knowing two opposed states, take refuge in the middle. Release both simultaneously, and reality shines in their interval.

padas: भावे त्यक्ते निरुद्धा चिन्नैव भावान्तरं व्रजेत्
iast: bhāve tyakte niruddhā cin naiva bhāvāntaraṃ vrajet
english: After one mental state is released, prevent awareness from moving to another. By abiding in the middle, a profound intuition unfolds.

padas: सर्वं देहं चिन्मयं हि जगद्वा परिभावयेत्
iast: sarvaṃ dehaṃ cin-mayaṃ hi jagad vā paribhāvayet
english: Contemplate the whole body—or the universe—as made entirely of consciousness. A simultaneous nonconceptual vision brings the supreme arising.

iast: vāyu-dvayasya saṃghaṭṭād antar vā bahir antataḥ
english: At the meeting of the two breaths, inwardly or outwardly, the yogin becomes receptive to the emergence of insight into equilibrium.

iast: sarvaṃ jagat sva-dehaṃ vā svānanda-bharitaṃ smaret
english: Remember the whole universe or your own body as filled with innate bliss. Simultaneous immersion in your own nectar makes awareness one with supreme bliss.
A deliberately enigmatic or 'trick' practice is said to evoke great bliss immediately; attend to that arising, through which reality is illuminated.
By restraining all channels and slowly raising prāṇa-śakti, attend to the ant-like tingling sensation; supreme happiness then expands.

padas: वह्नेर्विषस्य मध्ये तु चित्तं सुखमयं क्षिपेत्
iast: vahner viṣasya madhye tu cittaṃ sukha-mayaṃ kṣipet
english: Place the bliss-filled mind in the midst of fire and poison, or contemplate it filled only with breath; the verse links this intensity with the bliss of desire.

iast: yat sukhaṃ brahma-tattvasya tat sukhaṃ svākyam ucyate
english: At the culmination of Śakti's union, when the powerful absorption stirred by their meeting subsides, the experienced bliss is said to disclose brahman-reality.
Even without a partner, vividly remembering the fullness of erotic pleasure—kissing, embracing, and related contact—can release a flood of bliss.

padas: आनन्दे महति प्राप्ते दृष्टे वा बान्धवे चिरात्
iast: ānande mahati prāpte dṛṣṭe vā bāndhave cirāt
ānandam udgataṃ dhyātvā tal-layas tan-manā bhavet
english: When great joy arises, as on seeing a loved one after a long absence, meditate on the joy itself until mind dissolves into it.
As delight expands through eating and drinking, contemplate the state of fullness within the savour; from it, great bliss arises.
When the yogin becomes wholly one with the incomparable pleasure of music and similar experience, mind ascends into that and shares its nature.

iast: yatra yatra manas tuṣṭir manas tatraiva dhārayet
english: Wherever the mind finds genuine contentment, hold it precisely there. In that locus, the nature of supreme bliss unfolds.

padas: सावस्था मनसा गम्या परा देवी प्रकाशते
iast: sāvasthā manasā gamyā parā devī prakāśate
english: Attend to the threshold before sleep, when outer objects have vanished but sleep has not yet arrived; in that state Parā Devī becomes luminous.
Fix the gaze where sunlight, lamplight, or similar radiance mottles open space; sustained attention there reveals the form of the Self.
The verse names fierce Śakti-forms—Karaṅkiṇī, Krodhanā, Bhairavī, Lelihānā, and Khecarī—whose direct vision is associated with attainment of the supreme.

iast: nidhāya tat-prasaṅgena parā pūrṇā matir bhavet
english: Sit on a soft seat balanced on one buttock, leaving hands and feet unsupported; the resulting poised instability can open the mind into supreme fullness.

iast: kakṣa-vyomni manaḥ kurvañ śamam āyāti tal-layāt
english: Sit properly with the arms half-bent and place attention in the space of the armpits; as awareness merges there, it comes to rest.

padas: स्थूलरूपस्य भावस्य स्तब्धां दृष्टिं निपात्य च
अचिरेण निराधारं मनः कृत्वा शिवं व्रजेत्
iast: sthūla-rūpasya bhāvasya stabdhāṃ dṛṣṭiṃ nipātya ca
acireṇa nirādhāraṃ manaḥ kṛtvā śivaṃ vrajet
english: Fix an unmoving gaze on a gross form, then let the mind lose its support; quickly becoming groundless, it enters Śiva.

iast: hoccāraṃ manasā kurvaṃs tataḥ śānte pralīyate
english: With mouth open, place awareness at the tongue's centre and mentally utter the sound 'ha'; as the sound becomes peaceful, awareness dissolves.

iast: sva-dehaṃ manasi kṣīṇe kṣaṇāt kṣīṇāśayo bhavet
english: Whether seated or lying down, contemplate your body as unsupported. As mind diminishes, its stored dispositions are exhausted in a moment.

iast: praśānte mānase bhāve devi divyaugham āpnuyāt
english: While on a moving seat or gently rocking the body, attend when the mental state becomes tranquil; the verse says a divine current is then reached.

padas: आकाशं विमलं पश्यन् कृत्वा दृष्टिं निरन्तराम्
iast: ākāśaṃ vimalaṃ paśyan kṛtvā dṛṣṭiṃ nirantarām
stabdhātmā tatkṣaṇād devi bhairavaṃ vapur āpnuyāt
english: Gaze continuously into a spotless sky. When the inner activity becomes motionless, Bhairava's own form is immediately attained.

iast: līnaṃ mūrdhni viyat sarvaṃ bhairavatvena bhāvayet
english: Contemplate all space as dissolved into the crown and recognize it as Bhairava; then enter the luminous reality whose form is all Bhairava.

iast: kiṃcij jñātaṃ dvaita-dāyi bāhyālokas tamaḥ punaḥ
english: Ordinary partial knowing produces duality, while darkness conceals objects. Recognize both the visible world and darkness as Bhairava's form, bearing infinite light.

iast: taimiraṃ bhāvayan rūpaṃ bhairavaṃ rūpam eṣyati
english: On a very dark night during the waning moon, contemplate the form of darkness steadily; awareness comes to share Bhairava's form.

iast: prasārya bhairavaṃ rūpaṃ bhāvayaṃs tan-mayo bhavet
english: Close the eyes and first notice the dark appearance before them; then expand it while contemplating Bhairava's form, becoming one with it.
When any sense is obstructed and its activity stops, enter the resulting nondual void; there the Self shines by itself.

iast: abindum avisargaṃ ca a-kāraṃ japato mahān
english: Repeat the bare vowel 'a', without bindu or visarga. The verse says a great current of insight, identified with the supreme Lord, suddenly arises.
For a phoneme ending in visarga, place awareness at the visarga's termination; with a mind resting on no support, touch the timeless brahman.

iast: vyomākāraṃ svam ātmānaṃ dhyāyed digbhir anāvṛtam
nirāśrayā citiḥ śaktiḥ svarūpaṃ darśayet tadā
english: Meditate on the Self as sky-like, unbounded by the directions. Unsupported Citi-Śakti then reveals her own nature.

padas: तत्रैव चेतनां युक्त्वा भैरवे निर्मला गतिः
iast: tatraiva cetanāṃ yuktvā bhairave nirmalā gatiḥ
english: Pierce a small bodily part with a sharp needle or similar point and join awareness wholly to that sensation; concentrated attention opens a stainless way into Bhairava.
Contemplate inwardly: mental constructions, inner faculties, and the rest do not constitute my essence. With alternatives absent, awareness becomes free of them.

padas: माया विमोहिनी नाम कलायाः कलनं स्थितम्
iast: māyā vimohinī nāma kalāyāḥ kalanaṃ sthitam
ityādi-dharmaṃ tattvānāṃ kalayan na pṛthag bhavet
english: Reflect on Māyā as the power that deludes and on kalā as limitation or division. Understanding such tattvas as processes prevents taking them as truly separate.

iast: yata eva samudbhūtā tatas tatraiva līyate
english: Notice an impulse of desire the instant it arises and lead it into stillness; it dissolves back into the very source from which it emerged.

padas: यदा ममेच्छा नोत्पन्ना ज्ञानं वा कस्तदास्मि वै
iast: yadā mamecchā notpannā jñānaṃ vā kas tadāsmi vai
tattvato ’haṃ tathā bhūtas tal-līnas tan-manā bhavet
english: Ask: before desire or knowledge arises, who am I? Recognize that prior condition as your actual nature and let mind merge into it.

iast: icchāyām athavā jñāne jāte cittaṃ niveśayet
english: When desire or knowledge has arisen, place awareness directly within it and regard it as nonseparate from the Self; this reveals its essential meaning.

iast: tattvataḥ kasyacin naitad evaṃbhāvī śivaḥ priye
english: Knowing appears without an external cause or support and is therefore deceptive when treated as an owned object. Ultimately it belongs to no separate person; sustained recognition is Śiva-like.

iast: ataś ca tan-mayaṃ sarvaṃ bhāvayan bhava-jij-janaḥ
english: Consciousness is the common nature in every body, without intrinsic difference anywhere. Contemplating all as made of that consciousness carries one beyond becoming.

iast: buddhiṃ nistimitāṃ kṛtvā tat tattvam avaśiṣyate
english: Amid desire, anger, greed, delusion, pride, or envy, make awareness utterly still; when the agitation ceases, the underlying reality remains.

iast: bhramad vā dhyāyataḥ sarvaṃ paśyataś ca sukhodgamaḥ
english: Contemplate the entire world as magical display, a dispersed painting, or a spinning appearance. Seeing all phenomena in this way gives rise to ease.

padas: न चित्तं निक्षिपेद्दुःखे न सुखे वा परिक्षिपेत्
iast: na cittaṃ nikṣiped duḥkhe na sukhe vā parikṣipet
bhairavi jñāyatāṃ madhye kiṃ tattvam avaśiṣyate
english: Do not cast the mind into either pain or pleasure. Know the middle between them and ask what reality remains there.

padas: दृढेन मनसा दृष्ट्या नान्येक्षिण्या सुखी भवेत्
iast: dṛḍhena manasā dṛṣṭyā nānyekṣiṇyā sukhī bhavet
english: Release fixation on your own body and contemplate firmly, 'I am everywhere.' With unwavering, nonexclusive vision, spacious well-being arises.

iast: ghaṭādau yac ca vijñānam icchādyaṃ vā mamāntare
naiva sarvagataṃ jātaṃ bhāvayann iti sarvagaḥ
english: The awareness and impulses known in a pot or other object are not literally born within a separate 'me'; contemplate knowing as all-pervasive.
All beings share awareness of object and knower. The yogin's distinction is sustained attentiveness to the relation between them.

iast: apekṣāṃ sva-śarīrasya tyaktvā vyāpī dinair bhavet
english: Experience consciousness in another body as in your own. Gradually releasing dependence on your own body, become aware of pervasive consciousness.

iast: nirādhāraṃ manaḥ kṛtvā vikalpān na vikalpayet
english: Make the mind unsupported and do not construct further alternatives. In identity of individual self and supreme Self, Bhairava is recognized.

iast: sa evāhaṃ śaiva-dharmā iti dārḍhyād bhavec chivaḥ
english: Hold firmly: the omniscient, all-acting, all-pervading Supreme Lord is this very 'I.' Through conviction in these Śaiva qualities, one becomes Śiva-like.
As waves belong to water, flames to fire, and rays to the sun, the universe's diverse expressions are movements of Bhairava, not separate from the Self.

padas: भ्रान्त्वा भ्रान्त्वा शरीरेण त्वरितं भुवि पातनात्
iast: bhrāntvā bhrāntvā śarīreṇa tvaritaṃ bhuvi pātanāt
english: Spin the body repeatedly and then drop swiftly to the ground; when the agitating energy stops, a supreme state can arise in the sudden stillness.
Whether supports fail through incapacity, not-knowing, or dissolution of mind, attend to the end of the resulting surge; Bhairava's form appears there.

iast: sampradāyam imaṃ devi śṛṇu samyag vadāmy aham
english: Bhairava introduces a transmitted method: by simply holding the eyes completely still, kaivalya—unconditioned aloneness—is said to arise immediately.

padas: संकोचं कर्णयोः कृत्वा ह्यधोद्वारे तथैव च
iast: saṃkocaṃ karṇayoḥ kṛtvā hy adhodvāre tathaiva ca
anackam ahalaṃ dhyāyan viśed brahma sanātanam
english: Contract or close the ears and the lower gate, then meditate on the unvocalized, resonant sound; the practice leads into timeless brahman.
Standing over a deep well or great chasm, look upward; with a mind free from alternatives, mental activity dissolves clearly and at once.

padas: यत्र यत्र मनो याति बाह्ये वाभ्यन्तरेऽपि वा
iast: yatra yatra mano yāti bāhye vābhyantare ’pi vā
tatra tatra śivāvasthā vyāpakatvāt kva yāsyati
english: Wherever mind travels, outside or within, Śiva's state is already there. Since it is all-pervading, awareness has nowhere else to go.
Wherever consciousness manifests through a sensory path, attend to consciousness as the essence of that perception; dissolving into Citi brings fullness.

padas: क्षुताद्यन्ते भये शोके गह्वरे वा रणाद्द्रुते
iast: kṣutādy-ante bhaye śoke gahvare vā raṇād drute
english: At the end of a sneeze, fear, grief, confusion, flight from battle, curiosity, hunger, or similar interruption, a state grounded in brahman may flash forth.

iast: vastuṣu smaryamāṇeṣu dṛṣṭe deśe manas tyajet
english: When a remembered object appears, release the mind at the place where it was seen; making the body unsupported, the sovereign awareness expands.

iast: kvacid vastuni vinyasya śanair dṛṣṭiṃ nivartayet
taj jñānaṃ citta-sahitaṃ devi śūnyālayo bhavet
english: Rest the gaze on an object, then slowly withdraw it. As object-knowledge and mind recede together, awareness enters the abode of emptiness.

padas: सा शक्तिः शांकरी नित्यं भावयेत्तां ततः शिवः
iast: sā śaktiḥ śāṃkarī nityaṃ bhāvayet tāṃ tataḥ śivaḥ
english: The mind born from an upsurge of devotion in one free of attachment is Śaṅkarī Śakti; continually contemplate that power and become Śiva-like.

iast: tām eva manasā dhyātvā vidito ’pi praśāmyati
english: As one object is known through contrast with another, notice the emptiness gradually disclosed among things; meditating on that, even the known object becomes quiet.

iast: kiṃcij jñair yā smṛtā śuddhiḥ sāśuddhiḥ śambhu-darśane
na śucir hy aśucis tasmān nirvikalpaḥ sukhī bhavet
english: Ideas of purity taught by limited knowers are impure from Śambhu's vision. Beyond both pure and impure, remain nonconceptual and at ease.

iast: sarvatra bhairavo bhāvaḥ sāmānyeṣv api gocaraḥ
na ca tad-vyatirekeṇa paro ’stīty advayā gatiḥ
english: Bhairava's presence is everywhere, even in ordinary experience. Nothing exists outside it; this recognition is the nondual way.

padas: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च समो मानावमानयोः
iast: samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca samo mānāvamānayoḥ
brahmaṇaḥ paripūrṇatvād iti jñātvā sukhī bhavet
english: Remain equal toward enemy and friend, honour and dishonour. Knowing brahman as complete leaves no lack for preference to defend.

padas: न द्वेषं भावयेत्क्वापि न रागं भावयेत्क्वचित्
iast: na dveṣaṃ bhāvayet kvāpi na rāgaṃ bhāvayet kvacit
english: Cultivate neither aversion nor attachment. In the middle freed from both rāga and dveṣa, brahman expands into view.

iast: yad avedyaṃ yad agrāhyaṃ yac chūnyaṃ yad abhāva-gam
tat sarvaṃ bhairavaṃ bhāvyaṃ tad-ante bodha-sambhavaḥ
english: Whatever cannot be known or grasped, whatever is empty or marked by absence, contemplate all of it as Bhairava; awakening arises at that limit.
Place the mind in the outer sky—eternal, unsupported, pervasive, and beyond measurement—then enter the spaceless reality beyond even that support.

padas: यत्र यत्र मनो याति तत्तत्तेनैव तत्क्षणम्
iast: yatra yatra mano yāti tat tat tenaiva tat-kṣaṇam
english: Wherever mind goes, release that object in the very moment it appears. By refusing to settle anywhere, awareness becomes wave-free.
The verse gives an etymological contemplation of 'Bhairava' as the pervasive power that resounds through and sustains all; continuous recitation leads toward Śiva.
When notions such as 'I,' 'mine,' and 'this' arise, use them to direct meditation toward the unsupported ground into which mind can subside.
Meditate repeatedly on words such as eternal, all-pervading, unsupported, pervasive, and Lord of all; realize their meaning rather than treating them as labels.

iast: kiṃ tattvam indrajālasya iti dārḍhyāc chamaṃ vrajet
english: Regard the whole apparent world as insubstantial, like a magical illusion, and inquire firmly: what reality does an illusion possess? The mind comes to rest.

padas: आत्मनो निर्विकारस्य क्व ज्ञानं क्व च वा क्रिया
iast: ātmano nirvikārasya kva jñānaṃ kva ca vā kriyā
jñānāyattā bahirbhāvā ataḥ śūnyam idaṃ jagat
english: For the changeless Self, where could separate knowing or action reside? External objects depend upon knowing; seen thus, the world is empty of independent status.

padas: न मे बन्धो न मोक्षो मे भीतस्यैता विभीषिकाः
iast: na me bandho na mokṣo me bhītasyaitā vibhīṣikāḥ
pratibimbam idaṃ buddher jaleṣv iva vivasvataḥ
english: Recognize: there is no bondage or liberation belonging to me; these frighten only one who identifies with limitation. The world is a reflection in intelligence, like the sun in water.
Pleasure, pain, and other contacts arrive through the gates of the senses. Withdrawing investment from those gates, abide steadily in the Self.
Knowing illuminates all objects, while the Self illuminates every act of knowing. Because their essential luminosity is one, knower and known can be contemplated as nonseparate.

padas: यदा प्रिये परिक्षीणं तदा तद्भैरवं वपुः
iast: mānasaṃ cetanā śaktir ātmā ceti catuṣṭayam
yadā priye parikṣīṇaṃ tadā tad bhairavaṃ vapuḥ
english: When mind, consciousness, Śakti, and the separately conceived self have all subsided as distinct categories, what remains is Bhairava's form.

iast: dvādaśābhyadhikaṃ devi yaj jñātvā jñānavij janaḥ
english: Bhairava summarizes that 112 wave-free methods have been taught. Knowing them makes one a true knower of liberating insight.

padas: अत्र चैकतमे युक्तो जायते भैरवः स्वयम्
iast: atra caikatame yukto jāyate bhairavaḥ svayam
english: Established in even one of these methods, a practitioner becomes Bhairava-like; the verse then attributes extraordinary efficacy to such realization.
The description of attainment continues with freedom from aging and death, aṇimā and other powers, affinity with yoginīs, and mastery of their gatherings.

iast: jīvann api vimukto ’sau kurvann api na lipyate
idaṃ yadi vapur deva parāyāś ca maheśvara
english: Such a person is liberated while living and remains unstained while acting. The Goddess then begins asking about worship in light of this supreme embodiment.

padas: ध्यायते को महानाथ पूज्यते कश्च तृप्यति
iast: evamukta-vyavasthāyāṃ japyate ko japaś ca kaḥ
dhyāyate ko mahānātha pūjyate kaś ca tṛpyati
english: If reality is as described, the Goddess asks, who repeats what mantra, who meditates, and who worships or becomes satisfied?

padas: हूयते कस्य वा होमो यागः कस्य च किं कथम्
iast: hūyate kasya vā homo yāgaḥ kasya ca kiṃ katham
eṣātra prakriyā bāhyā sthūleṣv eva mṛgekṣaṇe
english: She continues: for whom is oblation offered, whose sacrifice is it, and how is it performed? Bhairava replies that ordinary procedure is external and suited to the gross level.

padas: भूयो भूयः परे भावे भावना भाव्यते हि या
जपः सोऽत्र स्वयं नादो मन्त्रात्मा जप्य ईदृशः
iast: bhūyo bhūyaḥ pare bhāve bhāvanā bhāvyate hi yā
japaḥ so ’tra svayaṃ nādo mantrātmā japya īdṛśaḥ
english: Here japa is repeatedly cultivating the supreme state. The mantra to be repeated is the self-arising inner resonance itself.

iast: dhyānaṃ hi niścalā buddhir nirākārā nirāśrayā
english: True dhyāna is steady intelligence without form or support, not a constructed image of body, eyes, face, hands, or other features.

padas: पूजा नाम न पुष्पाद्यैर्या मतिः क्रियते दृढा
निर्विकल्पे परे व्योम्नि सा पूजा ह्यादराल्लयः
iast: pūjā nāma na puṣpādyair yā matiḥ kriyate dṛḍhā
nirvikalpe pare vyomni sā pūjā hy ādarāl layaḥ
english: Pūjā is not essentially an offering of flowers; it is the firm, reverent dissolution of mind into the nonconceptual supreme space.
As fullness grows day by day through any one method, that very condition is tṛpti: satisfaction as complete and unsurpassable fullness.

iast: hūyate manasā sārdhaṃ sa homaś cetanāsrucā
english: True homa offers elements, senses, objects, and mind into the fire that dissolves within the great void, using consciousness itself as the ladle.
Here yāga is the contentment characterized by bliss; the verse begins explaining it through the destruction of limitation and protection of all.

padas: अन्यथा तस्य तत्त्वस्य का पूजा कश्च तृप्यति
iast: anyathā tasya tattvasya kā pūjā kaś ca tṛpyati
english: The explanation continues: entry into Rudra-Śakti is the sacred field, and supreme contemplation is the rite. Apart from this reality, who could worship or be satisfied?
The Self everywhere is essentially autonomous bliss and consciousness alone. Immersion of the self into that own-form is called the true ritual bath.

padas: यश्चैव पूजकः सर्वः स एवैकः क्व पूजनम्
iast: yair eva pūjyate dravyais tarpyate vā parāparaḥ
yaś caiva pūjakaḥ sarvaḥ sa evaikaḥ kva pūjanam
english: The offerings, the one receiving satisfaction, and the worshipper are all the same single reality. Where, then, can a separate act of worship remain?

iast: vrajet prāṇo viśej jīva icchayā kuṭilākṛtiḥ
english: Prāṇa moves outward and jīva enters along the intentionally curved, extended path of the great Goddess; she is the supreme field, both transcendent and immanent.

iast: tayā devyā samāviṣṭaḥ paraṃ bhairavam āpnuyāt
english: Remaining in accord with that Goddess within the sacrifice filled with great bliss, the practitioner is pervaded by her and reaches supreme Bhairava.

iast: sa-kāreṇa bahir yāti ha-kāreṇa viśet punaḥ
haṃsa-haṃsety amuṃ mantraṃ jīvo japati nityaśaḥ
english: Breath goes outward with 'sa' and enters again with 'ha'; every living being continuously repeats this natural haṃsa mantra.

padas: जपो देव्याः समुद्दिष्टः सुलभो दुर्लभो जडैः
iast: japo devyāḥ samuddiṣṭaḥ sulabho durlabho jaḍaiḥ
english: The spontaneous daily and nightly japa of the Goddess is counted as 21,600 breaths. It is easy because innate, yet hard to recognize for the unawakened.

padas: एतच्च नैव कस्यापि प्रकाश्यं तु कदाचन
iast: ity etat kathitaṃ devi paramāmṛtam uttamam
etac ca naiva kasyāpi prakāśyaṃ tu kadācana
english: Bhairava concludes that this supreme nectar has been taught, then frames it as an esoteric instruction not to be disclosed indiscriminately.
The caution continues: do not give the teaching to an unfit, cruel, or guru-disrespecting student; the verse turns toward the fearless, nonconceptual, inwardly mature.
The instruction may be given without hesitation to devoted members of the guru's lineage. The verse begins contrasting it with worldly possessions such as village, kingdom, family, and home.

padas: प्राणा अपि प्रदातव्या न देयं परमामृतम्
iast: sarvam etat parityajya grāhyam etan mṛgekṣaṇe
kim ebhir asthirair devi sthiraṃ param idaṃ dhanam
prāṇā api pradātavyā na deyaṃ paramāmṛtam
english: All unstable possessions may be relinquished for this enduring supreme treasure; even life may be given, yet the nectar-teaching should not be handed to the unqualified.
The Goddess declares herself completely satisfied: she has now grasped the essence of the Rudrayāmala Tantra.
Her concluding recognition continues: she now knows the heart of all the differentiated powers of Śakti.

padas: इत्युक्त्वानन्दिता देवी कण्ठे लग्ना शिवस्य तु
iast: ity uktvānanditā devī kaṇṭhe lagnā śivasya tu
english: Having spoken, the Goddess is filled with bliss and embraces Śiva; the text closes in the intimacy of their nondual communion.
